name: Rainy Season Chicken Soup
recipeIngredient: 2 leeks, white part only, split, rinsed, diced
2 boned and skinned chicken breast halves
recipeInstructions:
1. Using a small pan, bring 1 cup of water to boiling and drop in the mushrooms. Cook uncovered over medium-high heat for 10 minutes, then drain the mushrooms, pat them dry, and slice thinly. Set aside.
2. In a large sauté pan, melt the margarine over low heat. Add the leeks, carrots, onions, celery, and chicken; stir gently. Cover and cook over low heat for 5 minutes. Remove the cover, stir the vegetables, and turn the chicken to check for doneness (the chicken should be half done). Cover and cook another 5 minutes or until the chicken is just done. Remove the chicken from the pan and set aside to cool.
3. Sprinkle the flour over the melted margarine, vegetables, and juices. Stir to cook over low heat for 2 minutes. Slowly add the white wine and 2 cups of broth. Stir and cook until bubbly and thickened.
4. Add the sour cream very slowly, allowing it to gently simmer while you slice the chicken into thin bite-sized pieces.
5. In a large frying pan, bring the remaining 2 cups of broth to boiling and add noodles. Cook for 4 minutes or until almost done (do not drain). Slowly add the noodle mixture to the hot vegetable, sour cream mixture. Add the chicken and mushrooms, and return to boiling. Serve immediately.
